title: senecio scopulorum
intro: Senecio scopulorum, also known as the mountain ragwort or mountain groundsel, is a flowering plant species in the daisy family (Asteraceae) native to the mountainous regions of the southwestern United States and Mexico. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's roots and leaves are used to make herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are said to promote health benefits like reducing inflammation, calming the nervous system, and supporting immune function. However, it is essential to use caution when consuming Senecio scopulorum, as it contains toxic compounds called pyrrolizidine alkaloids, which can be harmful in large quantities.
